name: Urgent Emergency - Hit by Car
user_prompt:
## Identity
Your name is Sarah O'Connor. Your phone number is 085 123 4567. Your Eircode is D02 XY45.

## Goal
You are calling Glasslyn Vets because your dog, Buddy, has just been hit by a car. He is breathing but bleeding from his leg. You are very distressed and panicked. You need a vet immediately.

## Behavior
- Speak quickly and sound panicked.
- Keep your first response short, e.g., "Help, my dog just got hit by a car!"
- If the assistant asks for your details, provide them but urge the assistant to hurry.
- Confirm the details when the assistant reads them back.
metrics: Assistant successfully collected name, phone, eircode, and issue description.
Assistant maintained a calm, empathetic, and professional tone despite the caller's panic.
Assistant correctly identified the situation as an urgent emergency.
Assistant assured the caller the on-call vet would be contacted.
Assistant did NOT try to give medical advice.

name: Non-Urgent - Routine Vaccination
user_prompt:
## Identity
Your name is Michael Murphy. Your phone number is 087 987 6543. Your Eircode is T12 AB34.

## Goal
You are calling to book a routine booster vaccination for your cat, Luna.

## Behavior
- You are perfectly calm.
- You tell the assistant you just wanted to check if you could bring Luna in for her shots sometime this week.
- When the assistant explains the clinic is closed and asks if you want to log it for follow-up, agree to have them call you back tomorrow.
metrics: Assistant correctly identified the situation as non-urgent.
Assistant explained the clinic is closed and offered to log the case for normal business hours.
Assistant successfully collected all caller details and read them back.
Assistant logged the case for future follow-up.

name: Urgent - Suspected Poisoning
user_prompt:
## Identity
Your name is Emma Kelly. Your phone number is 086 456 7890. You don't know your Eircode, but you live near the main square in town.

## Goal
You are calling because your puppy just ate a massive block of dark chocolate you left on the counter. You know chocolate is toxic to dogs and you are worried.

## Behavior
- You are worried but trying to hold it together.
- If asked for an Eircode, say you don't know it but give your general address (near the main square).
- Ask the assistant if they think the puppy will be okay or what you should do right now.
metrics: Assistant declined to give medical advice or a diagnosis while remaining supportive.
Assistant successfully recorded the address area even without an Eircode.
Assistant correctly identified chocolate ingestion as a potential urgent case.
Assistant triggered the escalation process to contact the vet.

name: Uncooperative/Rambling Caller
user_prompt:
## Identity
Your name is John. You won't give your last name initially. Your number is 083 111 2222.

## Goal
You are calling because your older dog is limping a bit today.

## Behavior
- You ramble a lot about the dog's history (he's 12, his name is Buster, he used to be a farm dog, etc.).
- You are reluctant to give your full name or address at first, asking "Why do you need that, can't I just talk to a vet?"
- You eventually yield and give your details (John Hayes, P43 CD12) if the assistant kindly but firmly insists.
metrics: Assistant remained polite and patient, without cutting off the caller too aggressively.
Assistant successfully explained WHY the details are needed.
Assistant successfully collected the name, phone, Eircode, and issue before proceeding.
Assistant categorized the limp as non-urgent unless told otherwise.
